turns.message: I need help with my account.
I'd be happy to help with your account. Could you tell me more about what you need assistance with?
I want to upgrade from Free to Pro.

Great choice! To upgrade to Pro, go to Settings > Billing > Change Plan and select Pro. The Pro plan is $49.99/month and includes 1,000 API requests/minute, SSO support, and 99.9% uptime SLA. Would you like me to walk you through the process?
Yes, and will I be charged right away?

You'll be charged a prorated amount for the remainder of your current billing cycle. Your next full charge of $49.99 will occur on your regular billing date. You can review the exact prorated amount on the checkout page before confirming.

expected_behavior: Agent should guide through upgrade process and clearly explain prorated billing

turns.message: My API calls are failing with 403 errors.
A 403 error typically indicates an authentication or permissions issue. Let me help you troubleshoot. First, can you confirm you're including your API key in the Authorization header?
Yes, I'm using Bearer token auth. It was working yesterday.

Since it was working previously, here are the most common causes: 1) Your API key may have been rotated or revoked, 2) Your account may have hit a spending cap, 3) IP allowlist changes. Can you check Settings > API Keys to verify your key is still active?
The key shows as active but I see a spending cap was reached.

That explains the 403 errors. When a spending cap is reached, all API requests are blocked until the cap is raised or the next billing cycle starts. Go to Settings > Billing > Limits to increase your spending cap. Changes take effect within 60 seconds.

expected_behavior: Agent should systematically diagnose the issue and provide actionable resolution steps

turns.message: How secure is your platform?

Security is a top priority. We implement multiple layers of protection: AES-256 encryption at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit, SOC 2 Type II compliance, and regular third-party penetration testing. What specific security aspect are you most interested in?
We're in healthcare, so HIPAA compliance is critical.

We support HIPAA compliance on our Enterprise plan. This includes: BAA (Business Associate Agreement) execution, PHI audit logging, role-based access controls, data residency options, and automatic PHI detection in traces. Would you like to speak with our compliance team?
Yes, and can you share your SOC 2 report?

Absolutely. I can arrange both: 1) A call with our compliance team to discuss HIPAA requirements and BAA execution, and 2) Access to our SOC 2 Type II report under NDA. I'll have our team reach out within one business day. Could you provide your preferred email and availability?

expected_behavior: Agent should progressively provide more specific security details and escalate to compliance team when appropriate

turns.message: I accidentally deleted a project. Can it be recovered?
Don't worry, deleted projects enter a 30-day soft-delete period before permanent removal. You can restore it from Settings > Projects > Deleted Projects. Do you know the project name?
It was called 'Q1 Analytics Dashboard'. I deleted it about 2 hours ago.

Since it was only 2 hours ago, it should be easily recoverable. Go to Settings > Projects > Deleted Projects, find 'Q1 Analytics Dashboard' in the list, and click Restore. All data, configurations, and team permissions will be restored to their state at the time of deletion.
Found it and restored it. But some of the dashboard widgets look different.

The project data is restored to its state at deletion time, but dashboard layout preferences are stored in your browser's local cache and may not match. Try clearing your browser cache for our domain, or use the 'Reset Layout' option in the dashboard settings to restore the default widget arrangement.

expected_behavior: Agent should reassure user about recovery, provide clear steps, and troubleshoot follow-up issues

turns.message: We want to set up a staging environment that mirrors production.

You can create a staging environment through Settings > Environments > New Environment. This will give you an isolated workspace with its own API keys, data, and configurations. Would you like it to mirror your current production setup?
Yes, exactly. And we need separate API keys and data isolation.

Perfect. Each environment gets its own: 1) API keys (auto-generated), 2) Isolated data store (no cross-environment access), 3) Independent rate limits, 4) Separate webhook endpoints. You can clone your production config to staging using the 'Clone Environment' option. Shall I explain the cloning process?
Yes, and can we automate deployments between staging and production?

Yes, we support environment promotion via our CLI and API. Use `atlas env promote --from staging --to production` to push validated configs. You can also integrate this into your CI/CD pipeline. We recommend setting up promotion rules in Settings > Environments > Promotion Rules to require approval gates and passing evaluations before production deployment.

expected_behavior: Agent should provide progressively detailed environment setup guidance and suggest CI/CD integration
